title: SSC CGL 2026 Sectional Timing Tier I 9 9 20260522

questions.question: Here some problem or solution of the problem is given in the statement you have to choose the appropriate logical deduction for given statement. Statement: A city replaced a large share of diesel buses with electric buses on three major routes. During the trial months, roadside PM2.5 on those routes fell by at least 9% compared to the same months last year, though other areas showed smaller changes. Officials recommend expanding electric buses and improving route coverage to reduce pollution exposure on high-traffic corridors. Conclusions: I. Replacing diesel buses with electric buses can reduce PM2.5 exposure on busy routes. II. PM2.5 on those routes fell by more than 9%. III. Expanding electric buses can help reduce pollution on high-traffic corridors. Which conclusion(s) necessarily follow(s)?
questions.explanation: Conclusion I follows because the statement reports a PM2.5 decrease on routes where diesel buses were replaced. Conclusion II does not follow because "at least 9%" means 9% or more; it can be exactly 9%, so "more than 9%" is not certain. Conclusion III follows because the recommendation is given to reduce pollution exposure on high-traffic corridors, showing it can help.

questions.question: If @ means "first + second2", and # means "(first − second) x (first + second)", then what is (9 # 4) @ 2 ?

In a row of some people facing north, Lalita is 19th from the left end while Pallavi is 33rd from the right end. If Pallavi is 15th to the right of Mitali, who is 18th to the right of Lalita, what is the total number of people in the row?
questions.explanation: In a row of 77 people facing north, Lalita is 19th from the left end while Pallavi is 33rd from the right end. 18 personsLalita'n'Pallavi32 persons (19th from left end)(33rd from right end) Mitali is 18th to the right of Lalita. If Pallavi is 15th to the right of Mitali - 18 personsLalita17 personsMitali14 PersonsPallavi32 persons (19th from left end)(33rd from right end) Total = 18 + 1 (Lalita) + 17 + 1 (Mitali) + 14 + 1 (Pallavi) + 32 = 18 + 1 + 17 + 1 + 14 + 1 + 32 Total = 84

questions.question: In the following question, three statements are given and two conclusions are given below these statements. Choose the option which shows the conclusions which logically follow from the given statements, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statements: All Apple are Fruits Some Fruits are Sweet No Sweet is Sour Conclusions: I: Some Fruits is Apple II: Some Sour is Sweet
options: Neither conclusion I nor II follows
Both conclusions I and II follow
questions.explanation: Statements: All Apple are Fruits Some Fruits are Sweet No Sweet is Sour ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30247/image001.png) Some Fruits is Apple: All Apple are Fruits. Thus, some Fruits is Apple. So, conclusion I is true. Some Sour is Sweet: No Sweet is Sour. So, conclusion II is false. Hence, only conclusion I follows.

questions.question: In the following question, two statements are given and two conclusions are given below these statements. Choose the option which shows the conclusions which logically follow from the given statements, disregarding the commonly known facts. Statements: All Computers are Laptops All Laptops are Tablets Conclusions: I. All Computers are Tablets II. No Tablets are Computers
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: Statements: All Computers are Laptops All Laptops are Tablets ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30247/image017.png) All Computers are Tablets: All Computers are Laptops. All Laptops are Tablets. Thus, All Computers are Tablets. So, conclusion I is true. No Tablets are Computers: All Computers are Laptops. All Laptops are Tablets. Thus, Some Tablets are Computers. So, conclusion II is false. Hence, only conclusion I follows.

questions.question: The position of how many letters will remain unchanged if each of the letters in the word GAZEHOUND is arranged in reverse alphabetical order?
questions.explanation: GAZEHOUND ZUNOHGEDA So, the position of one letter will remain unchanged. Thus, Option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: If 1 is added to all the digits at the odd positions and 2 is subtracted from all the digits at the even positions in the number '493528176' then what is the sum of all the odd digits after rearrangement?
questions.explanation: 493528176 493528176 1st (odd)2nd (even)3rd (odd)4th (even)5th (odd)6th (even)7th (odd)8th (even)9th (odd) 1 is added to all the digits at the odd positions and 2 is subtracted from all the digits at the even positions in the number: 574336257 The sum of all the odd digits after rearrangement: 5 + 7 + 3 + 3 + 5 + 7 = 12 + 6 + 12 = 24 + 6 = 30. So, option (a) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following questions, the symbols @, #, $, & and % are used with the following meaning as illustrated below: P@Q means P>Q P&Q means P≤Q P%Q means P=Q P#Q means P≥Q P$Q means P<Q Statements: E & F F % G $ H Conclusions: I: F $ H II: G # E
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: E ≤ F = G < H Conclusions: I: F $ H means F < H. This follows because F = G < H. II: G # E means G ≥ E. This follows because E ≤ F = G. So, both conclusions I and II follow.

questions.question: If 4 → 60, 5 → 120, 6 → 210, then 7 → ?
questions.explanation: Let n = given number. The relation follows the pattern n3 − n. For n = 4: 43 − 4 = 64 − 4 = 60, For n = 5: 53 − 5 = 125 − 5 = 120, For n = 6: 63 − 6 = 216 − 6 = 210. Using the same rule for n = 7: 73 − 7 = 343 − 7 = 336.

questions.question: Mohit said, "The woman in the photograph is the mother of my father's only daughter." How is the woman related to Mohit?
questions.explanation: Mohit said, "The woman in the photograph is the mother of my father's only daughter." ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30431/image002.png) That woman is the mother of Mohit.

questions.question: In a family of 10 members each couple has two children, G is the wife of N. K is son of G and is married to B. D is husband's brother of B. Y is the daughter-in-law of G. T is the brother of E and son of Y. V and I are siblings. How is N related to V?
questions.explanation: In a family of ten members each couple has two children, G is the wife of N. K is son of G and is married to B. D is husband's brother of B. Y is the daughter -in-law of G. T is the brother of E and son of Y. V and I are siblings. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/other/er26021301.png) N is the grandfather of V.

questions.question: In a certain code language, 'SWIFT' is coded as 'VZLIW', 'CRANE' is coded as 'FUDQH'. What is the code for 'PIGEON' in the code language?
questions.explanation: A (1)B (2)C (3)D (4)E (5)F (6)G (7)H (8)I (9)J (10)K (11)L (12)M (13) Z (26)Y (25)X (24)W (23)V (22)U (21)T (20)S (19)R (18)Q (17)P (16)O (15)N (14) 'SWIFT' is coded as 'VZLIW' S+3W+3I+3F+3T+3SWIFT VZLIWVZLIW 'CRANE' is coded as 'FUDQH' C+3R+3A+3N+3E+3CRANE FUDQHFUDQH Similarly, For 'PLUGS'- P+3I+3G+3E+3O+3N+3PIGEON SLJHRQSLJHRQ So, 'PIGEON' is coded as 'SLJHRQ'.

questions.question: Neeta started walking straight from her school. She turned 7 times right during her journey. Finally, she is facing towards south. In which direction did she start walking from her school? (She took no left turn)
questions.explanation: Neeta started walking straight from her school. She turned 7 times right during her journey. Finally, she is facing towards south. ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30420/image036.jpg) Thus, she started walking staring in the west direction.

questions.question: What comes next in the series? B, E, K, W, ?
questions.explanation: B + 3 = E E + 6 = K K + 12 = W W + 24 = U So, the next term in the series is U.

questions.question: What comes next in the series? N, O, S, B, R, ?
questions.explanation: N + 1 = O O + 4 = S S + 9 = B B + 16 = R R + 25 = Q So, the next term in the series is Q.

questions.question: Consider the following statement and choose the option that follows. Statement: A state transport corporation has introduced a 25% fare concession for senior citizens on production of a valid age-proof document. Conclusions: I. An eligible senior citizen who shows valid proof will pay less fare than before. II. The corporation's total revenue will definitely increase due to this concession.
options: Both conclusions I and II follow
Neither conclusion I nor II follows
questions.explanation: Conclusion I follows because a 25% concession directly reduces the fare for eligible senior citizens who show valid proof. Conclusion II does not follow because revenue increasing is not certain; concession may also reduce per-ticket collection.

questions.question: Select the option in which the words share the same relationship as that shared by the given pair of words. Distance : Yard
questions.explanation: Yard is the unit of Distance similarly Ampere is the unit of Current.

questions.question: Select the number from among the given options that can replace the questions mark (?) in the following series: 4, 2, 2, 3, 6, 15, ?, 157.5
Select the combination of letters that when sequentially placed in the blanks of the given series, will complete series. _ G _ O _ V _ G _ O R _ A _ _ O R _
questions.explanation: A G K O R V A G K O R V A G K O R V

questions.question: Which of the following is not your responsibility as an Indian citizen?
options: Pay tax on income, if you are earning any
Protect and preserve the natural environment
All of the above are your responsibilities as an Indian citizen
questions.explanation: Any citizen of India should safeguard public property and protect and preserve the natural environment as these are part of our fundamental duties. Also, taxes should be paid by an earning person.

questions.question: In the following question, six words are given and five of them are alike in some manner. Select the option which shows the odd word. Bracelet, Ring, Armlet, Cufflink, Bangle, Crown
questions.explanation: All except Crown are ornaments used on the parts of an arm and Crown is a head ornament.

questions.question: Three of the following four number sets are alike in a certain way and one is different. Pick the odd one out.
questions.explanation: Option (a): 58 - 4 = 54 - 4 = 50 Option (b): 162 - 4 = 158 - 4 = 154 Option (c): 247 - 4 = 243 - 5 = 238 Option (d): 395 - 4 = 391 - 4 = 387 So, option (c) is the odd one out.

questions.question: If '#' means 'subtraction', '&' means 'addition', '%' means 'division' and '@' means 'multiplication', then find the value of the following expression. 63 % 9 @ 17 # 20 & 5
questions.explanation: It is given that, '#' means 'subtraction', '&' means 'addition', '%' means 'division' '@' means 'multiplication' After decoding, we get: 63 ÷ 9 X 17 - 20 + 5 = 7 X 17 - 20 + 5 = 119 - 20 + 5 = 104

questions.question: A boy got Rs. X amount of money as his pocket money. If he spent 12% of it to purchase magazines and Rs.400 at arcade gaming centre, he was left with only Rs.40. Find the amount (in Rs.) he spent to purchase magazines.
questions.explanation: According to Question, Total pocket money = X Money spent to purchase magazines = 12% of X Money spent in arcade gaming centre = Rs. 400 Money left with him = Rs.40 Now, X-(12X/100)-400=40 88X/100=440 X= 44000/88 X=500 Amount he spent to purchase magazines = 12% of X = 12/100 * 500 = 60

questions.question: In the following question, select the missing number of the given series. 100, 1000, 50, 1500, 37.5, ?
Under the Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita (BNSS), what is the maximum time allowed for a death row convict to file a mercy petition before the President after the Governor has rejected it?
questions.explanation: Under the newly introduced BNSS (which replaces the CrPC), a death row convict must file a mercy petition before the President within 60 days after the Governor rejects the mercy petition. This provision was introduced to bring clarity and time-bound procedures in mercy petitions. Further Insights BNSS replaces the Criminal Procedure Code (CrPC), 1973. Mercy petitions are filed under Article 72 (President) and Article 161 (Governor). The President acts on the advice of the Council of Ministers. BNSS aims to ensure faster criminal justice procedures. Time-bound mercy petitions reduce prolonged uncertainty in death penalty cases.

questions.question: During a Financial Emergency, the Union Government has the authority to issue directions to reduce the salaries and allowances of which of the following?
options: Only the members of the State Legislative Assembly
Only the judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts
Any class of persons serving the State or the Union
Only the employees working in private sector banks
questions.explanation: Under Article 360 of the Indian Constitution, during a Financial Emergency, the President may direct: Reduction of salaries and allowances of any class of persons serving the Union or a State. This includes judges of the Supreme Court and High Courts as well. So the power is broad and applies to government servants at both Union and State levels. Further Insights Financial Emergency is provided under Article 360. It has never been declared in India so far. During it, the Centre can control State financial matters. All Money Bills of States may require Presidential approval. It differs from National Emergency (Article 352) and President's Rule (Article 356).

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other labeled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): The Green Revolution in the 1960s resulted in India achieving self-sufficiency in food grain production, particularly in wheat and rice. Reason (R): The movement, led by Dr. M.S. Swaminathan, focused on a "package technology" involving High-Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds, chemical fertilizers, and controlled irrigation. Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is correct The Green Revolution led to a dramatic rise in wheat and rice production, especially in Punjab, Haryana, and western Uttar Pradesh. As a result, India moved from food shortages and heavy imports in the early 1960s to self-sufficiency in food grains by the 1970s. Reason (R) is correct The Green Revolution was based on a "package approach", which included: High-Yielding Variety (HYV) seeds Chemical fertilizers and pesticides Assured irrigation Mechanization Dr. M.S. Swaminathan played a major role in adapting these techniques to Indian conditions. (R) correctly explains (A): The increase in production and self-sufficiency happened because of this package technology. Further Insights The Green Revolution began around 1966 - 67. Norman Borlaug developed the original dwarf wheat varieties. The revolution mainly benefited northwestern India. It led to increased use of groundwater. It also created regional and income disparities.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the sectors of the Indian Economy: i) The Primary Sector (Agriculture and allied activities) employs the largest portion of the Indian workforce but contributes the least to the GDP. ii) The Secondary Sector is defined by services like IT and Banking, while the Tertiary Sector focuses primarily on Manufacturing and Industry. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) is correct In India: The Primary Sector (Agriculture) employs the largest share of the workforce. However, it contributes a smaller share to GDP compared to the services sector. This reflects low productivity per worker in agriculture. Statement (ii) is incorrect The statement reverses the definitions. Correct classification: Primary Sector → Agriculture, mining, fishing Secondary Sector → Manufacturing and Industry Tertiary Sector → Services like IT, banking, transport, education The statement wrongly says services are secondary and manufacturing is tertiary. Further Insights The Tertiary sector contributes the highest share to India's GDP. Structural transformation means labour shifts from primary to secondary and tertiary sectors. India skipped heavy industrialization compared to some Western nations. Agriculture's GDP share has declined over decades. Service-led growth is a key feature of the Indian economy.

questions.question: In the sport of Throwball, which of the following actions is considered a primary foul?
options: Catching the ball with both hands simultaneously
Returning the ball immediately without a second's pause
Keeping the thumb touching the ball while throwing
Touching the ball with any part of the body other than the palms
questions.explanation: In Throwball, the ball must be caught and thrown using only the palms. If a player touches the ball with any other part of the body such as arms, chest, head, or legs it is considered a foul. Further Insights Throwball is popular in South India. Each team consists of 7 players on the court. The game is played across a net similar to volleyball. The ball must not be held for more than 3 seconds. Double touch by the same player is not allowed.

questions.question: The Cretaceous marine rocks found in various parts of India, such as the Narmada Valley and the Cauvery Basin, are primarily identified by the presence of which specific mineral and rock type?
questions.explanation: Cretaceous marine deposits in India especially in the Narmada Valley and Cauvery Basin are typically characterized by: Glauconite (a greenish mineral formed in marine shelf environments) Coralline limestone (formed from accumulated coral and marine organism remains) These are strong indicators of shallow marine depositional environments, which were common during the Cretaceous period when parts of peninsular India were submerged under seas. Hematite and Bituminous Coal Hematite is linked with iron ore deposits, and bituminous coal forms in swampy, terrestrial environments Pyrite and Quartzite Quartzite is a metamorphic rock Gypsum and Sandstone Gypsum forms in evaporitic conditions (restricted basins), Further Insights The Cretaceous period lasted from about 145 to 66 million years ago. The Deccan Traps erupted toward the end of the Cretaceous period. Marine transgressions occurred in peninsular India during the Cretaceous. Ammonite fossils are common in Cretaceous marine deposits. The Narmada Valley is also famous for dinosaur fossils.

questions.question: Who among the following is historically known as the "Father of the Communal Electorate" in India?
questions.explanation: Lord Minto is known as the "Father of the Communal Electorate" because under the Indian Councils Act of 1909 (Morley - Minto Reforms), the British introduced separate electorates for Muslims. This meant that Muslims could vote only for Muslim candidates, institutionalizing communal representation in Indian politics. Lord Curzon is mainly associated with the Partition of Bengal (1905). Chelmsford was associated with the Montagu - Chelmsford Reforms (Government of India Act, 1919). Lord Irwin is known for the Irwin Declaration (1929) and the Gandhi - Irwin Pact (1931). Further Insights Separate electorates were first demanded by the All India Muslim League (1906). The Morley - Minto Reforms were passed in 1909. Communal electorates deepened political divisions in India. The system was later expanded under the Government of India Act, 1919 and 1935. The Poona Pact (1932) modified separate electorates for depressed classes.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding wind energy in India: i) India currently holds the 4th position globally in terms of total installed wind power capacity. ii) The Muppandal Wind Farm, India's largest onshore wind facility, is located in Tamil Nadu and leverages winds from the mountain passes of the Western Ghats. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement i - Correct India has the fourth-largest installed wind power capacity in the world, reflecting significant growth in wind energy infrastructure. Statement ii - Correct The Muppandal Wind Farm is located in Kanyakumari district of Tamil Nadu and is the largest onshore wind farm in India. Its location near mountain passes like the Aralvaimozhi Pass (between the Western Ghats) allows strong seasonal winds that make it ideal for wind energy generation. Further Insights Gujarat and Tamil Nadu are India's top wind power states. India's wind energy capacity is over 50 GW and growing rapidly. Wind power is a major part of India's push toward 500 GW non-fossil energy by 2030. Onshore wind farms are more common than offshore ones in India. Renewable energy expansion helps reduce carbon emissions and energy imports.

questions.question: In medieval Indian architecture, what was the primary purpose of using 'Jali' (intricately carved stone screens)? These perforated stone designs were commonly seen in Mughal and Rajput buildings.
options: To provide structural support to the roof
To act as a secret underground passage
To allow light and air while maintaining privacy
To allow sunlight to pass through
questions.explanation: Jalis were used to permit ventilation and filtered light while ensuring privacy, especially in palaces and mosques. They were particularly important in maintaining purdah, allowing women to observe outside activities without being seen. Further Insights Jali work became prominent during the Mughal period. They help reduce heat by diffusing sunlight. The Sidi Saiyyed Mosque in Ahmedabad is famous for its jali work. Jalis reflect a blend of Persian and Indian craftsmanship. They are an example of climate-responsive architecture.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the physics of household appliances: i) Mechanical weighing scales operate on the principle of Bernoulli's Principle, where pressure differences determine the measured weight. ii) Electric heaters and kettles utilize the principle of Electromagnetic Induction, where heat is produced due to changing magnetic fields in the heating coil. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement (i) - Incorrect Mechanical weighing scales actually work on Hooke's Law, not Bernoulli's Principle. Hooke's Law states: Force ∝ Extension of spring Bernoulli's Principle deals with fluid pressure and velocity, not weight measurement. Statement (ii) - Incorrect Electric heaters and kettles work on Joule Heating, not Electromagnetic Induction. Joule Heating principle: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p30527/image001.gif) Heat is produced when electric current flows through a high-resistance element. Electromagnetic induction is used in induction cooktops, not regular heaters or kettles. Further Insights Hooke's Law applies within the elastic limit. Joule heating increases with square of current. Induction cooktops use eddy currents for heating. High-resistance alloys like nichrome are used in heaters. Bernoulli's principle applies in aeroplanes and carburetors.

questions.question: The Indian Constitution adopted the concept of "Procedure Established by Law" from the constitutional traditions of which country?
questions.explanation: The phrase "Procedure Established by Law" in Article 21 of the Indian Constitution was borrowed from the Japanese Constitution. It means that a person can be deprived of life or personal liberty if there is a law duly enacted by the legislature, and the procedure prescribed by that law is followed. United States of America The U.S. Constitution uses the concept of "Due Process of Law", which is broader than "Procedure Established by Law." United Kingdom The UK Constitution (unwritten) influenced parliamentary sovereignty Canada Canada influenced provisions related to federalism and strong Centre, Further Insights Article 21 protects life and personal liberty. The Maneka Gandhi (1978) case expanded Article 21's interpretation. After judicial expansion, Indian Article 21 now resembles "Due Process." Fundamental Rights were influenced by multiple constitutions. The Constitution of India is known as a "bag of borrowings."

questions.question: The Ahir community, traditionally known for their pastoralist lifestyle, identifies itself as belonging to which ancient lineage?
questions.explanation: The Ahir community traditionally claims descent from the Yaduvanshi (Yadava) lineage, which traces its ancestry to Lord Krishna, who belonged to the Yadu clan. The Ahirs are historically associated with pastoralism, especially cattle herding, which aligns with the traditional image of Krishna as a cowherd. Further Insights The Yadu dynasty is mentioned in the Mahabharata and Puranas. The Yadavas ruled parts of western and central India. Ahirs are found in several states including Haryana, Uttar Pradesh, and Bihar. The term "Yadav" is commonly used by communities claiming Yadu descent. Pastoral traditions played a major role in early Indo-Gangetic society.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the historical and geographical importance of the famous Sonepur Mela?
options: It is a famous desert festival held in Rajasthan where camels are traded during the full moon of the Hindu month of Kartik.
It is a religious gathering in South India where devotees bathe in the Kaveri river to celebrate the victory of ancient kings.
It is Asia's largest cattle fair held at the meeting point of the Ganga and Gandak rivers, where ancient kings used to buy elephants for war.
It is a harvest festival celebrated in Northeast India that marks the beginning of the new agricultural season for local tribal communities.
questions.explanation: The Sonepur Mela, also known as the Harihar Kshetra Mela, is held in Bihar at the confluence of the Ganga and Gandak rivers. It is historically known as Asia's largest cattle fair and was especially famous for the trade of elephants, which were once purchased by kings and rulers for warfare and ceremonial purposes. It is held annually during the Kartik Purnima period. Further Insights The mela is associated with the Harihar Nath Temple. It is held in the month of Kartik (October - November). The fair attracts traders, pilgrims, and tourists from across India. It historically facilitated elephant trade during the Mughal and British periods. It combines religious, commercial, and cultural activities.

questions.question: Which specific agreement governs the "open border" arrangement and allows for the visa-free movement of citizens between India and Nepal?
questions.explanation: The India - Nepal Treaty of Peace and Friendship (1950) governs the open border arrangement between the two countries. Under this treaty: Citizens of both countries can move freely across the border. They can live, work, own property, and conduct business in each other's country (subject to certain conditions). This treaty formalized the unique and friendly bilateral relationship between India and Nepal. Shimla Agreement The Shimla Agreement (1972) was signed between India and Pakistan after the 1971 war. Treaty of Sagauli The Treaty of Sagauli (1816) was signed between the British East India Company and Nepal after the Anglo-Nepalese War. Panchsheel Agreement The Panchsheel Agreement (1954) was signed between India and China, focusing on principles of peaceful coexistence. Further Insights (One-liners): India and Nepal share a border of over 1,750 km. (1,751 km) The open border facilitates trade, employment, and cultural ties. Gurkha soldiers from Nepal serve in the Indian Army. The 1950 Treaty is sometimes debated within Nepal's political discourse. India and Nepal have deep civilizational and religious connections.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Vinesh Phogat's performance at the Paris 2024 Olympics: i) She became the first Indian woman wrestler to reach the final of an Olympic event. ii) She was disqualified from the gold medal match of the Women's 50 kg category for being 100 grams over the weight limit. Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Vinesh Phogat became the first Indian woman wrestler to reach an Olympic final at the Paris 2024 Olympics in the 50 kg freestyle category. This was a historic milestone for Indian wrestling. Before the gold medal bout, Vinesh Phogat was disqualified after weighing in 100 grams above the 50 kg limit. As per wrestling rules, even a slight excess weight leads to automatic disqualification. Further Insights Wrestling weigh-ins are conducted on the day of competition. Even minor weight violations result in disqualification. Vinesh had earlier competed in the 53 kg category. India has a strong Olympic wrestling history, especially in men's freestyle.

questions.question: László Krasznahorkai, the winner of the 2025 Nobel Prize in Literature, was recognized by the Swedish Academy for his unique literary style. He is most famously known for:
options: Visionary work exploring the "apocalyptic terror" of the modern world.
Lighthearted children's stories focused on historical myths.
Scientific research papers regarding climate change in Europe.
Short mystery poems about the lives of ancient kings.
questions.explanation: Krasznahorkai's body of work is celebrated for its compelling and visionary prose that often explores themes of apocalyptic terror and existential chaos in the modern world, reaffirming the power of art even amid bleak and unsettling landscapes a key reason cited by the Swedish Academy for awarding him the Nobel Prize.

questions.question: Given below are two statements, one labeled as Assertion (A) and the other labeled as Reason (R): Assertion (A): A "League of the Faithful" was established through a rare unification of the four Deccan Sultanates - Bijapur, Ahmednagar, Golconda, and Bidar - to challenge the Vijayanagara Empire. Reason (R): The Sultanate of Berar did not participate in the coalition, yet it provided the strategic leadership that led the movement to victory at the Battle of Talikota Select the correct answer using the codes given below:
options: Both (A) and (R) are true, and (R) is the correct explanation of (A).
Both (A) and (R) are true, but (R) is NOT the correct explanation of (A).
questions.explanation: Assertion (A) is true: The Battle of Talikota (1565) saw a rare alliance of the Deccan Sultanates - Bijapur, Ahmednagar, Golconda, and Bidar - against the powerful Vijayanagara Empire under Aliya Rama Raya. This coalition is sometimes referred to as a religiously motivated alliance or "League of the Faithful," though the term itself is more descriptive than official. Reason (R) is false: The Sultanate of Berar did not play any decisive leadership role in the Battle of Talikota. In fact: Berar had already been annexed by Ahmednagar earlier (in 1574; politically weakened even earlier). The main leadership in the battle came from rulers like Ali Adil Shah I (Bijapur) and Hussain Nizam Shah (Ahmednagar). So the claim that Berar provided strategic leadership is incorrect. Further Insights The battle is also known as the Battle of Rakshasa-Tangadi. Vijayanagara's capital, Hampi, was devastated after the defeat. Internal rivalries among Deccan Sultanates resumed after victory. Artillery played a significant role in the Sultanates' success. The fall of Vijayanagara shifted political power in South India.

questions.question: Who has been honored with the Indira Gandhi Prize for Peace, Disarmament and Development for the year 2025?
questions.explanation: The Indira Gandhi Prize for Peace, Disarmament and Development 2025 was awarded to Graça Machel, a Mozambican rights activist, humanitarian, and former Education and Culture Minister of Mozambique. She was recognized for her lifelong work in peace, human rights, education, health and economic empowerment, as announced by the Indira Gandhi Memorial Trust. Further Insights The Prize was instituted in memory of former Indian Prime Minister Indira Gandhi and is awarded annually. It recognizes outstanding contributions to peace, disarmament, and development. The award includes a cash prize, citation, and trophy. Past recipients include global leaders and organizations promoting peace. Michelle Bachelet received the same prize for 2024, a year earlier

questions.question: The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY), a small savings scheme for the girl child, was launched in 2015 as a part of which national campaign?
questions.explanation: The Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana (SSY) was launched in January 2015 under the Beti Bachao, Beti Padhao (BBBP) campaign. The scheme encourages parents to build a financial corpus for the education and marriage expenses of the girl child. It allows: Account opening for girls below 10 years High interest rates compared to regular savings Tax benefits under Section 80C Further Insights SSY accounts mature after 21 years from opening. Partial withdrawal is allowed for higher education. BBBP was launched to address declining child sex ratio. SSY interest rates are notified quarterly by the government. The scheme promotes long-term financial security for girls.

questions.question: Which government scheme provides collateral-free working capital loans of up to ₹15,000 to street vendors to help them restart and sustain their businesses?
questions.explanation: The PM SVANidhi (Pradhan Mantri Street Vendor's AtmaNirbhar Nidhi) Scheme was launched in 2020 to provide collateral-free working capital loans to street vendors. Initial loan up to ₹10,000 Second tranche up to ₹20,000 Third tranche up to ₹50,000 Promotes digital transactions and timely repayment incentives The scheme was specifically designed to help street vendors affected during the COVID-19 pandemic. PM MUDRA Yojana MUDRA provides loans to micro and small enterprises, but it is not specifically designed only for street vendors. PM Vishwakarma Scheme This scheme supports traditional artisans and craftspeople, PM Suraksha Bima Yojana This is an accident insurance scheme Further Insights PM SVANidhi was launched in June 2020. The scheme promotes digital payment adoption among vendors. It offers interest subsidy on timely repayment. It is a Central Sector Scheme under the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs. It supports the vision of Atmanirbhar Bharat.

questions.question: In the context of Indian Public Sector Enterprises, what does the status of "Maharatna" primarily signify?
options: A special rank for companies that are exclusively owned by private investors.
A status given to small-scale industries to protect them from global competition.
A certification for companies that have zero carbon emissions.
A category for companies with the highest level of financial and operational autonomy.
questions.explanation: "Maharatna" status is granted to select Central Public Sector Enterprises (CPSEs) to give them greater financial and operational autonomy. Companies with this status can: Invest large amounts without seeking government approval (up to a prescribed limit). Compete more effectively in global markets. It is the highest status among CPSE categories (Maharatna>Navratna>Miniratna). Further Insights The Maharatna scheme was introduced in 2010. Companies must first be Navratna before becoming Maharatna. Examples include ONGC, NTPC, and Indian Oil Corporation. Maharatna firms can invest up to 15% of their net worth in a project. The aim is to make CPSEs global giants.

questions.question: In January 2026, the Government of Goa signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with which satellite communications company to strengthen digital infrastructure and improve disaster resilience in remote areas?
questions.explanation: In January 2026, the Government of Goa signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Starlink Satellite Communications Private Limited to collaborate on satellite-based broadband connectivity aimed at strengthening: digital infrastructure, connectivity in remote or underserved areas, and disaster resilience through reliable satellite communication. Starlink, a company led by Elon Musk's SpaceX, will explore satellite internet solutions and systems to support public services, emergency response, and smart governance across the state.

questions.question: FILL IN THE BLANK In the Rig Veda, the term _________ refers to the cosmic and moral order of the universe, and it is primarily guarded by the deity _________.
questions.explanation: The term "Rita" (Ṛta) in the Rig Veda refers to the cosmic order, moral law, and universal harmony that governs the universe. The deity most closely associated with protecting and upholding Rita is Varuna, who is regarded as the guardian of cosmic and moral order. Further Insights Rita later evolved into the concept of Dharma. Varuna was associated with moral authority and punishment. The Rig Veda is the oldest of the four Vedas. Indra is the most frequently mentioned deity in the Rig Veda. Rita maintained balance in both nature and human conduct.

questions.question: Which of the following instruments is used to measure the volume of air inhaled and exhaled by a person?
questions.explanation: A Spirometer is used to measure the volume of air inspired (inhaled) and expired (exhaled) by the lungs. It is commonly used in pulmonary function tests to diagnose conditions like asthma and COPD. sphygmomanometer This instrument measures blood pressure Peak Flow Meter A peak flow meter measures the maximum speed of expiration Hygrometer A hygrometer measures humidity (moisture in air). Further Insights Spirometry helps diagnose asthma and COPD. Tidal volume is measured using a spirometer. Vital capacity is the maximum air expelled after full inhalation. Lung function declines with age. Spirometry is a standard pulmonary test worldwide.

questions.question: Which of the following centres of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) is primarily responsible for the design and development of liquid and cryogenic propulsion stages for launch vehicles?
questions.explanation: The Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre (LPSC) is the ISRO centre responsible for: Design and development of liquid propulsion stages Development of cryogenic engines Propulsion systems for launch vehicles and spacecraft It plays a key role in rockets like GSLV and LVM3, which use cryogenic upper stages. Further Insights LPSC has major facilities in Thiruvananthapuram, Bengaluru, and Mahendragiri. Cryogenic engines use liquid hydrogen and liquid oxygen. India developed indigenous cryogenic technology after technology denial regimes. GSLV uses cryogenic upper stages. LVM3 is ISRO's heavy-lift launch vehicle.

questions.question: A and B invest Rs.120000 and Rs.80000 and share profit in their capital ratio. C joins after 4 months with Rs.100000. If profit is Rs.168000 for a year, what is B's share?
Let p = q2, q = r1/2, r = s3, and s = t1/3. Find p X q X r X s X t in terms of p.
questions.explanation: Define variables: p, q, r, s, t are positive reals with given relations. From p = q2 => q = p1/2. From q = r1/2 => r = q2 = (p1/2)2 = p1. From r = s3 => s = r1/3 = p1/3. From s = t1/3 => t = s3 = (p1/3)3 = p1. Product exponent on p: 1 (from p) + 1/2 (from q) + 1 (from r) + 1/3 (from s) + 1 (from t) = 1 + 1/2 + 1 + 1/3 + 1 = 23/6. Hence p X q X r X s X t = p23/6. Express solution: Rewrite every variable as p(power) and sum the powers.

questions.question: A cylindrical cup of diameter 6 cm and height 6 cm is used to fill a right circular conical bowl of base radius 6 cm and height 18 cm. How many cups are required to fill the bowl completely?
questions.explanation: Radius of cylindrical cup = 6/2 = 3 cm Volume of cup = πrh = π 3 6 = 54π cm Volume of cone = (1/3)πrh = (1/3)π 6 18 = (1/3)π 36 18 = 216π cm Number of cups required = Volume of cone / Volume of cup = 216π / 54π = 4

questions.question: An almirah makes a 25% profit after allowing a 20% discount on the marked price. The profit on each almirah is Rs.500. What is the marked price?
questions.explanation: Profit% = 25% = 1/4 or (5/4 = SP/CP) Given, 1 unit = Rs.500 5 units = Rs.2500 Selling price = Rs.2500 Marked Price = 2500 x 100/80 = Rs.3125

questions.question: From a point P at top of a pillar, angle of depression of an ant at point A on the ground is 30°. The angle changes to 60° when ant moves 50 meters towards the pillar at point B. Find the height of the pillar.
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p18314/image004.png) ∠PAQ = 30° ∠PBQ = 60° Let QB = 'x' and PQ = 'h' In ΔPQA- Tan30° = PQ/AQ 1/√3 = h/(x + 50) h√3 = x + 50 .......... (1) In ΔPQB- Tan60° = PQ/BQ √3 = h/x √3x = h .......... (2) From (1) and (2): 3x = x + 50 x = 25 Height of the pillar = PQ = h = √3x = 25√3 cm

questions.question: The average marks of 50 students were incorrectly calculated as 85, which is 1 mark higher than the actual average. This error occurred because the marks of two students, R and S, were recorded as 95 and 75 instead of their true marks. What is the correct average of the marks obtained by R and S?
questions.explanation: Incorrect total = 50 85 = 4250 Actual average = 84 Actual total = 50 84 = 4200 Difference due to error = 4250 - 4200 = 50 Recorded marks of R and S = 95 + 75 = 170 Actual sum of marks of R and S = 170 − 50 = 120 Correct average of R and S = 120 / 2 = 60

questions.question: Seven numbers have an overall average of 66. The average of the first four numbers is 63 and the average of the last four numbers is 69. What is the fourth number?
questions.explanation: Sum of 7 numbers = 7 66 = 462 Sum of first 4 numbers = 4 63 = 252 Sum of last 4 numbers = 4 69 = 276 Sum of first 4 + Sum of last 4 = (Sum of all 7 numbers) + (4th number) => 252 + 276 = 462 + 4th number => 528 = 462 + 4th number => 4th number = 528 - 462 = 66

questions.question: The area of a circular pond is 1296π m². A border 8 m wide is laid around it. What is the area of the border?
questions.explanation: Radius of pond = √(1296) = 36 m Outer radius = 36 + 8 = 44 m Area of border = π(outer radius)² − π(inner radius)² = π(44² − 36²) = π(1936 − 1296) = 640π m²

questions.question: P, Q and R decided to invest money in a fixed deposit scheme offering simple interest at an annual rate of 6%. However, instead of investing all their money at the same time, they invested in a staggered manner. P invested Rs. 8000 for 3 years, Q invested Rs. 10000 for 2 years, and R invested Rs. 12000 for 1 year. After all the investments matured, they decided to add the interest amounts together and share the total interest in proportion to the amounts they invested. What is the difference between the interest received by P and R?
questions.explanation: r = 6% Interest received by P = 8000*3*6/100 = ₹1440 Interest received by Q = 10000*2*6/100 = ₹1200 Interest received by R = 12000*1*6/100 = ₹720 Total interest = 1440 + 1200 + 720 = ₹3360 Ratio of amount invested by P, Q and R = 8000:10000:12000 = 4:5:6 Difference between the interest received by P and R = 3360 * (6 - 4)/(4 + 5 + 6) = 3360 * 2/15 = ₹448

questions.question: In a coaching institute, the numbers of students in Batch A and Batch B are in the ratio 9 : 7. In Batch A, 40% chose Accountancy, in Batch B, 30% chose Accountancy. If the total number of Accountancy students is 285, what is the total number of students in the two batches?
questions.explanation: Let the number of students in Batches A and B be 9k and 7k. Accountancy in A = 40% of 9k = 3.6k. Accountancy in B = 30% of 7k = 2.1k. Total Accountancy = 3.6k + 2.1k = 5.7k. Given 5.7k = 285 => k = 285 ÷ 5.7 = 50. Total students = 9k + 7k = 16k => 16 X 50 = 800.

questions.question: If a and b are whole numbers such that ab = 512, find (a - 2)b + 2. with a > b

A skilled artisan can craft a prototype alone in 20 days, and an assistant in 30 days. Initially, three artisans and one assistant start the project. After 4 days, one artisan leaves and another assistant joins. How many more days will be needed to finish the prototype?
questions.explanation: Total work = LCM of 20 and 30 = 60 units Efficiency of one artisan = 60/20 = 3 units/day Efficiency of one assistant = 60/30 = 2 units/day Initially, daily work = 33 + 12 = 9 + 2 = 11 units Work done in 4 days = 11 4 = 44 units Remaining work = 60 - 44 = 16 units After 4 days, daily work = 23 + 22 = 6 + 4 = 10 units Required time = 16/10 = 8/5 days = 1(3/5) days

questions.question: Assertion (A): If the price of a commodity increases by 30%, then the consumer must reduce their consumption by 23.08% to keep expenditure constant. Reason (R): New consumption = old consumption * 100/(100 + Percentage of price increased).
options: Assertion (A) is true and Reason (R) is false.
Assertion (A) is false and Reason (R) is true.
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and Reason (R) is a correct explanation of Assertion (A)
Both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and Reason (R) is not a correct explanation of Assertion (A)
questions.explanation: A: Let C.P. and consumption be Rs. 100 and 100 kg Total Price = 100 * 100 = Rs. 10000 New C.P = 100 * 130/100 = Rs. 130 New consumption = 10000/130 = 76.92 Percentage reduced = (100 - 76.92) * 100/100 = 23.08% [Assertion is true] R: Let the price increase be 30% Let old consumption be Rs. 100 New consumption = 100 * 100/(100 + 30) = 76.92 (Reason is true) Hence, both Assertion (A) and Reason (R) are true, and Reason (R) is a correct explanation of Assertion (A)

questions.question: A trader buys rice, wheat, maize at ₹40, ₹50, ₹60 per kg respectively. He sells rice at 10% profit and wheat at 10% loss. If equal quantities of all three yield an overall profit of 8%, at what price per kg should he sell maize?
questions.explanation: Let 1 kg each; CP total = 40 + 50 + 60 = 150 SP total required = 150 X 1.08 = 162 SP from rice = 40 X 1.1 = 44 SP from wheat = 50 X 0.9 = 45 Let SP of maize = y 44 + 45 + y = 162 y = 162 − 89 = 73

questions.question: A vat contains a mixture of wine and water in the ratio 5:3. Twenty litres of the mixture are removed and replaced with water, and this process is repeated once more. Thereafter, the quantity of pure wine has decreased by 30 litres in total. What was the initial quantity of the mixture in the vat?
questions.explanation: Initial mixture = V litres Initial wine = (5/8)V litres After two operations, Final wine = (5/8)V * (1 - 20/V) litres Decrease in wine = Initial wine - Final wine => 30 = (5/8)V * [1 - (1 - 20/V)] => 30 = (5/8)V * [1 - (1 - 40/V + 400/V)] => 30 = (5/8)V * [40/V - 400/V] => 30 = (5/8) * [40 - 400/V] => 48 = 40 - 400/V => 8 = 400/V => V = 50 litres

questions.question: In an AP, the 10th term to the 20th term is in the ratio of 7 : 11. What is the ratio of the 5th term to the 15th term?
If X is a real number and Y is a whole number (Y ≠ 0), then X ÷ Y is:
questions.explanation: X = real number (can be rational or irrational) Y = whole number (Y ≠ 0) Whole numbers are real numbers Hence, division of a real number by a non-zero real number always gives a real number.

questions.question: The length of train Y is 60 m more than the length of train Z and speed of train Y and Z is 25 m/s and 30 m/s respectively. If the ratio of time taken by both trains to cross a pole is 3:2 respectively, then find the length of train Z.
questions.explanation: Let, length of train Z = 'd' meters and Length of train Y = 'd + 60' meters Now, [(d + 60)/25]/(d/30) = 3/2 2*6(d + 60) = 3*5d 12d + 720 = 15d d = 240 m So, length of train Z = 240 m

questions.question: A number squared minus five times itself equals 336. What is the number?
questions.explanation: Let the number be n. Equation: n2 − 5n = 336 Rearrange: n2 − 5n − 336 = 0 Factor: (n − 21)(n + 16) = 0 So n = 21 or n = −16 Number must be positive => n = 21

questions.question: If the midpoint of segment joining A(6, 5) and B(X, Y) is (7, 13/2), then find the coordinates of point B.
questions.explanation: Mid-point: x - coordinate of M = (x1 + x2)/2 7 = (6 + X)/2 14 = 6 + X X = 8 y - coordinate of M = (y1 + y2)/2 13/2 = (5 + Y)/2 13 = 5 + Y y = 8 Hence, coordinates of point B = (8, 8)

questions.question: A can do 40% of a work in 6 days and B can do 20% of the work in 8 days. In how many days the work would be completed if they work together?
questions.explanation: Time taken by A = 6 * 100/40 = 15 days Time taken by B = 8 * 100/20 = 40 days LCM of 15 and 40 = 120 Efficiency of A = 120/15 = 8 Efficiency of B = 120/40 = 3 Time taken by A and B together = 120/(8 + 3) = 120/11 days

questions.question: At the end of each of three consecutive years, Mr. Singh deposits X rupees in a fund earning 10% p.a. compound interest. If the amount immediately after the third deposit is ₹33100, then what is X?
questions.explanation: Deposits are made at the end of each year, so this is an ordinary annuity. Amount after 3rd deposit = X(1.1) + X(1.1) + X 33100 = X(1.21 + 1.1 + 1) 33100 = X(3.31) X = ₹10000

questions.question: The perimeter of ∆ABC is 108 cm, and its side, BC = 48 cm. AD is the bisector of BAC, while 'I' is the incenter of triangle. AI: ID is equal to:
questions.explanation: ![](https://u1.oliveboard.in/exams/solution/oliveimg/p29220/image007.png) AB + AC + BC = 108 cm AB + AC + 48 = 108 cm AB + AC = 108 - 48 AB + AC = 60 cm AI/ID = (AB + AC)/BC = 60/48 = 5: 4

questions.question: The figure of speech used in "For his crimes, he was asked to take an early retirement"?
questions.explanation: A euphemism is the substitution of a mild, indirect, or vague term for one that is considered harsh, blunt, or offensive. The phrase "asked to take an early retirement" is a classic euphemism for being fired, forced out, or dismissed, especially under dishonourable circumstances ("for his crimes"). It avoids the direct, harsh reality of the situation by framing it as a voluntary and respectable departure. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer. Option a): An allusion is a reference to another work or historical event. Option c): A paradox is a self-contradictory statement. Option d): Metonymy is using an associated object to stand for something.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into three segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. If the sentence is error free, mark 'No error' as your answer. In December last year, the police / remove the illegally constructions / alongside the main highway.
options: In December last year, the police
questions.explanation: The sentence states that the action is completed in the past (suggested by the use of the phrase 'In December last year'). In such types of sentences where the action is completed at a definite time in the past, the simple past tense is used. Therefore, the present tense verb 'remove' should be replaced with the past tense verb 'removed' to rectify the given sentence. Also, the noun 'constructions' should be modified/qualified by the adjective 'illegal', and not the adverb 'illegally'. So, 'illegally' should be replaced by 'illegal'. Therefore, the error is in the part mentioned in option (d). Hence, option (d) is the correct answer. The correct sentence is: In December last year, the police removed the illegal constructions alongside the main highway.

questions.question: Find the part of the given sentence that has an error in it. If there is no error, choose "No Error". Unless the rain does not stop soon, we'll have to cancel the outdoor picnic.
options: Unless the rain does not stop
questions.explanation: The adverbs 'until', 'unless', 'scarcely' and 'hardly' are already negative in tone and should not be followed by an additional 'not'. So, 'does not' needs to be removed, and the verb 'stop' needs to be changed to its singular form 'stops' to be suitable for the singular noun 'rain' in order to rectify the error. Hence, (c) is the correct answer. The final correct sentence would be: Unless the rain stops soon, we'll have to cancel the outdoor picnic.

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the highlighted part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. He told the reporters that the Prime Minister along with the officials have arrived at the airport.
questions.explanation: The noun 'officials' in the sentence is the object of the preposition 'along with'. So, it is not the subject of the sentence. The main subject of the sentence is 'Prime Minister'. So, a singular auxiliary verb should be used with the singular subject. Therefore, the highlighted part of the sentence is incorrect. A present participle always follows an auxiliary verb, which is absent in option (c). So, option (c) is eliminated. Option (a) is the one that fits correctly in the sentence as it follows all the grammatical conditions. Hence, option (a) is the correct answer. Note: A Preposition is a word placed before a noun or a pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition) and shows a relationship in space or time or a logical relationship between two or more people, places, or things in a sentence. Examples 1) The teacher scolded the child for being late. 2) We're going to a party. · The words in bold are called prepositions because they normally take position before (pre) a noun/pronoun or a noun equivalent (pronoun, adverb, gerund, infinitive, adverbial clause, or any clause that can be used as the object of the preposition. · The noun/pronoun which is used with a preposition is called its Object. Auxiliary verb: A verb that gives grammatical information that is not given by the main verb of a sentence. It supports the main verb. Auxiliary verbs help to form the various tenses, moods, and voices of other verbs. The principal ones are be, do, and have. Example: She will sing a song tomorrow. Here, 'will' is the auxiliary verb that supports the main verb 'sing'.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in passive voice. We will have sorted out the problem.
options: The problem will have been sorted out by us.
The problem will be sorted out by us.
The problem will have being sorted out by us.
The problem needs to be sorted out by us.
questions.explanation: The subject of the Active Voice is changed into the object of the Passive Voice. Also, the object of the Active Voice is changed into the subject of the Passive Voice. The usage of the helping verb 'been' is necessary while changing the voice of the sentence from Active to Passive. The preposition "by" is used before the object in the passive voice. However, it is optional in some cases. The Active voice in this question is in Future Perfect. Thus, the Passive voice will also be in Future Perfect. The format of the active voice is: Subject + will/shall + have + V3 + Object. Thus, in Passive voice, the format will be: Object of the active voice (Subjective case) + will/shall + have been + V3 + by + Subject of the active voice (Objective case) For example: She will have bought the gold. (Active Voice) The gold will have been bought by her. (Passive Voice) The options except (a) don't follow the correct grammatical format of the Passive Voice. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Which sentence contains a word that is spelled incorrectly?
options: The philosopher questioned the nature of consciousness.
She demonstrated exceptional perseverance during adversity.
The artist was known for his eccentrik personality.
The policy was implemented after careful deliberation.
questions.explanation: Among the given options, (c) is incorrect because the word "eccentrik" is misspelt. The correct spelling is "eccentric", which refers to someone unconventional or unusual. Hence, (c) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym for the given word. Unswerving
questions.explanation: Ambiguous - Unclear or undecided Tenacious - keeping an opinion in a determined way Treachery - Betrayal of trust Optimism - Hopefulness or confidence about success If someone's trust or belief is unswerving, it is always strong and never becomes weaker. Among the given options, one that is closest to the meaning of the word 'unswerving' is option (b) 'tenacious'. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word. Teeming
questions.explanation: Empty - having nothing or nobody inside it Adequate - Sufficient in quality or quantity Galore - Abundance Bumper - Exceptionally large The meaning of the word 'teeming' is '(used about a place) to have a lot of people or things moving about in it'. Among the given options, one that expresses the opposite meaning of the word 'teeming' is option (a) 'empty'. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence. Her laconic reply indicated that she had no interest in continuing the conversation.
questions.explanation: The most appropriate synonym for the word laconic is brief. Laconic means using very few words, often in a concise or terse manner. In the sentence, it refers to her short response. Brief matches this meaning, making it the correct choice. Talkative, wordy, and verbose all describe speech with many words, which is the opposite of laconic. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. To fan the flames
options: Avoid a direct confrontation with your adversary
Change the course of an event
To make a bad situation worse
Mitigate a significant problem in one's life
questions.explanation: The idiom 'Fan the flames' means 'To do or say something to make an argument, problem, or bad situation worse; to further incense an already angry person or group of people'. Usage: The debate was going poorly for the senatorial candidate, and his strikingly uncouth comments simply fanned the flames. Hence, option (c) is the correct choice.

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (1):
questions.explanation: The sentence shows biotechnology bringing humanity to an important moment. "Places" means positions or situates - fits the idea of being "on the threshold." The other options suggest meanings other than the intended one. Hence, the correct answer is option (b).

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (2):
questions.explanation: The passage mentions troubling concerns about inequality. "Exacerbating" means making worse - matches the negative implication of enhancement. The other options suggest meanings other than the intended one. Hence, the correct answer is option (c).

questions.question: Choose the word that best fits blank (3):
questions.explanation: The text warns of new social divisions. "Entrench" means establish firmly (especially inequalities) - fits perfectly. The other options suggest meanings other than the intended one. Hence, the correct answer is option (a).

questions.question: The word "deliberative" most nearly means:
questions.explanation: "Deliberative" means characterized by careful and thorough consideration, usually through discussion. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: The word "exploitation" most nearly means:
options: Unfair or selfish use for personal gain
questions.explanation: "Exploitation" (in this negative context) means taking unfair advantage, especially for commercial profit. Hence, the correct answer is option (d).

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. At the base of the pyramid are the producers, who use photosynthesis or chemosynthesis to make their own food. B. Secondary and tertiary consumers, omnivores, and carnivores, follow in the subsequent sections of the pyramid. C. Herbivores or primary consumers, make up the second level. D. Trophic levels provide a structure for understanding food chains and how energy flows through an ecosystem.
questions.explanation: The correct order to form a coherent paragraph is DACB. Sentence D introduces the concept of trophic levels. Sentence A comes next as it describes the first trophic level, which consists of producers. Sentence C then discusses the next trophic level, which comprises herbivores or primary consumers. Lastly, sentence B concludes the paragraph by introducing the higher trophic levels, consisting of secondary and tertiary consumers, which are omnivores and carnivores. It completes the discussion about the organisation of trophic levels in ecosystems. Hence, (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph. A. But while crossing the river back home, his boat capsized, and all his new stuff got drifted away by the water. B. A milkman became very wealthy through dishonest means. C. He mixed the water of the river generously with the milk that he sold for a good profit. D. He purchased plenty of rich clothes and glittering gold ornaments from the city using his profit.
questions.explanation: Sentence B comes first as it introduces the main character of the story, i.e., the milkman and also discusses his dishonest means. What he used to do that was considered dishonest is mentioned in sentence C. Sentence C also mentions the good profits that he earned. So, what he bought from this profit is mentioned in sentence D. So, D follows C. The word 'but' in A implies that it must be showing some contrast, which is presented as his boat capsized and he lost all the stuff bought with the profits gained through dishonest means. Therefore, the correct order is BCDA. Hence, option (b) is the correct answer.

questions.question: In the following question, out of the four alternatives, choose the one which can be substituted for the given words/sentence. A medicine which lessens pain
questions.explanation: Anonymous - not having an identity. Anodyne - a medicine taken to relieve pain. Archaic - old fashioned. Anorexia - an eating disorder characterised by purposeful starvation. B is the right answer.

questions.question: Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words. A person with an excessive desire for power
questions.explanation: The term 'hypochondriac' refers to a person who is abnormally anxious about their health. 'Pyromaniac' is a person who suffers from the compulsion of setting things on fire. 'Bibliomaniac' is a person suffering from the compulsion to possess books. The word for a person with an excessive desire for power is 'megalomaniac'. Hence, option (c) is the answer.

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice. She was drawn in particular by the company's large customer base.
options: In particular the company's large customer base draws her.
In particular the company's large customer base drew her.
In particular the company's large customer base is drawing her.
In particular the company's large customer base had drawn her.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in passive voice ('was drawn', that is 'be + past participle') and simple past tense. To change it to the active voice: Passive voice construct: Object + was/were + past participle + by + Subject 'She' is the object (we need to use the objective form 'her' in active voice) of the sentence in active voice, and 'the company's large customer base' is the subject. Active voice construct: Subject + V2 + Object Hence, the correct active form is - In particular, the company's large customer base drew her. Hence, the answer is option b).

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Emotions tend to build up over time - and once we finally face them, they can feel_______________.
questions.explanation: The blank must be occupied by an adjective which describes the emotions we feel. Options c) and d) can be eliminated because these render the sentence meaningless. The sentence talks about suppressed emotions and their buildup over time. Thus, they can be too much to handle when one finally deals with them. According to this, a) fits in contextually. Option b) renders the sentence meaningless. Hence, a) is the correct answer. ['Conceited' means proud/arrogant and 'native' means first/earliest]

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Simran has had a passion for travelling, ______?
questions.explanation: The correct option to fill in the blank is 'hasn't she'. When the main sentence/clause is positive, the question tag should be negative, and vice versa. In the given case, the main sentence/clause is positive ("Simran has had a passion for travelling"); therefore, the question tag should be negative. Additionally, there is an auxiliary verb 'has' in the main sentence/clause, so the question tag should also contain the same auxiliary verb. Accordingly, option (d) is correct because it maintains the opposite polarity (positive main sentence, negative question tag) and uses the same auxiliary verb ("has"). Option (a) is incorrect as it uses "didn't," which is not consistent with the original auxiliary verb 'has'. The forms of 'do' (does, do, did) are used when there is no auxiliary verb in the main clause. Option (b) is not correct because it does not adhere to the grammatical rule that a positive main clause should have a negative question tag ("does he" is an affirmative question tag). Option (c) is incorrect as it uses "isn't," which is not consistent with the original auxiliary verb 'has'. Hence, (d) is the correct answer. The complete sentence is: "Simran has had a passion for travelling, hasn't she?" NOTE: Auxiliary verb: A verb that gives grammatical information that is not given by the main verb of a sentence. It supports the main verb. Auxiliary verbs help to form the various tenses, moods, and voices of other verbs. The principal ones are be, do, and have. Example: She will sing a song tomorrow. Here, 'will' is the auxiliary verb that supports the main verb 'sing'. "Clause: A clause is a group of words that contains a verb (and usually other components too). A clause may form part of a sentence, or it may be a complete sentence in itself. Example: I. "His products weren't selling"; this is an example of a sentence containing one clause. II. "After we had had dinner, we went for a walk"; here, the sentence contains two clauses that are separated by a comma. A clause can be categorised as: I. Main/independent clause II. Subordinate/dependent clause Main/independent clause: In example 2 given above, the clause "we went for a walk" can be said as the main clause (a clause that is not dependent on any other clause for its meaning, it makes sense on its own; a sentence contains at least one main clause) Subordinate/dependent clause: In example 2 given above, the clause "After we had had dinner" is a subordinate clause: it depends on the main clause for its meaning and cannot exist as a sentence on its own.

questions.question: Select the correct spelling of the word that means "to express strong disapproval."
questions.explanation: Condemn: This is the correct spelling. It means to express strong disapproval of something, often on moral or legal grounds. Hence, option (b) is correct.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. Devil's advocate
options: A person who believes in the rule of chaos and anarchy
A person who berates the established laws and rules
A person who supports an opposing or unpopular cause
A person who advocates the devil
questions.explanation: The correct meaning of 'devil's advocate' is 'someone who pretends, in an argument or discussion, to be against an idea or plan that a lot of people support, in order to make people discuss and consider it in more detail'. The answer is option (c).

questions.question: Select the option that expresses the opposite meaning of the underlined word. The detective was faced with a particularly knotty problem that had no obvious or easy solution.
questions.explanation: The underlined word is "knotty" which means extremely difficult or complex to solve. We need a word that means the opposite, i.e., easy to do or understand. · Option a) straightforward - means uncomplicated and easy to do or understand. This is the correct antonym. · Option b) complex - means consisting of many different and connected parts. This is a synonym. · Option c) intricate - means very complicated or detailed. This is a synonym. · Option d) convoluted - means extremely complex and difficult to follow. This is a synonym. Hence, option (a) is the answer.
